title: Petco Health and Wellness (NASDAQ: WOOF) Q2 2023 Earnings Summary
text: Petco Health and Wellness (NASDAQ: WOOF) Q2 2023 Earnings Summary. Financial Results: Petco missed earnings expectations in Q2 2023, reporting adjusted EPS of $0.06 versus expectations of $0.07. Net revenue reached $1.5 billion, representing 3% year-over-year growth with comparable sales also up 3%. Key Performance Drivers: Services excelled, growing 17% year-over-year and reaching a $700+ million run rate. The company ended Q2 with 269 veterinary hospitals and expects to approach 300 by year-end. Digital sales grew 9%, while brick-and-mortar growth was 2%. However, discretionary categories struggled. The supplies and companion animals businesses declined 9% during the quarter, pressured by consumer spending constraints. Consumables showed resilience with 7% growth. Revised Guidance: Management lowered full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance to $460-480 million (from prior expectations) and adjusted EPS to $0.24-0.30. CEO Ron Coughlin stated the company is not yet where I want us to be in translating top line growth to the bottom line. Strategic Actions: Petco announced a comprehensive cost-reduction program targeting $150 million in run rate cost savings by the end of fiscal 2025, with $40 million expected in year one. This includes approximately 25% headcount reductions in corporate and field leadership roles. The company is reintroducing premium brands like Fancy Feast to drive traffic while addressing consumer value-seeking behavior through targeted pricing actions.
